---
label: "AI Control with Human Oversight"
icon: "🤝"
---

## Agentic SAP: AI-Driven Operations with Human Guardrails

The endgame isn't AI understanding legacy code — it's AI **operating** on SAP systems autonomously within defined boundaries, with human oversight for high-risk decisions.

### The Autonomy Spectrum

<ComparisonTable title="AI Autonomy Levels for SAP" columns={['Level','AI Does','Human Does','Risk','Example']} rows={[
  ['Level 0: Manual','Nothing','Everything','None','Traditional ABAP development'],
  ['Level 1: Assist','Explains code, suggests changes','Reviews, decides, implements','Low','Joule Explain + Generate'],
  ['Level 2: Guided','Implements changes','Reviews and approves','Medium','Claude Code with plan mode'],
  ['Level 3: Supervised','Executes autonomously within rules','Monitors, intervenes on exceptions','Medium-High','Agentic ABAP with approval gates'],
  ['Level 4: Autonomous','Full lifecycle management','Sets policies, handles escalations','High','Future: AI-managed SAP operations'],
]}/>

### Building Approval Gates

<CodeBlock language="python" label="LangGraph Human-in-the-Loop for SAP Changes" b64="" />

Notice the **two human gates**: one after AI explains the code (validate understanding) and one after AI proposes changes (approve modifications). This ensures AI never modifies production SAP code without human oversight.

<AnalogyBox emoji="🏭" title="Change Advisory Board for AI">
In ITIL, a Change Advisory Board (CAB) reviews and approves changes before they reach production. The approval gates in our LangGraph pipeline serve the same purpose: AI proposes, humans review, tests verify, then and only then does the change enter the transport system. The AI accelerates the work; the human ensures the safety.
</AnalogyBox>

### SAP Agents Architecture (2026 Roadmap)

SAP's own agentic architecture is emerging: agents that interact through an ABAP backbone, with ABAP MCP server exposing capabilities to external agents. This enables a multi-agent approach:

<CodeBlock language="text" label="Multi-Agent SAP Modernization" b64="" />

<Quiz
  question="An AI agent proposes modernizing a custom SAP pricing module. Unit tests pass. Should you deploy directly to production?"
  options={["Yes — tests passing means it's safe", "No — pass through the transport system with CAB approval, integration testing in QA, and staged rollout", "Only if it's a minor change", "Deploy to production but monitor closely"]}
  correctIndex={1}
  explanation="Passing unit tests is necessary but insufficient for production SAP changes. The full governance chain applies: transport system (version control), integration testing (does it work with other modules?), QA validation (business process testing), CAB approval (change management), staged rollout (landscape progression). AI accelerates the development; it doesn't bypass governance."
/>

<SeeItInRe3 text="Re³'s debate pipeline uses the same pattern: agents do the work, Atlas (quality gate) evaluates, and the human user makes final decisions about which synthesis to accept." />
